message:
perf(lexical): raise CASS_MAX_WRITER_THREADS to 32, batch adds via Writer::run, lift bulk-load merge threshold to 256, expose CASS_TANTIVY_MAX_WRITER_THREADS env override

Three coupled changes that target the cass-compat rebuild path on
many-core servers without regressing workstation behaviour.

CASS_MAX_WRITER_THREADS: 8 → 32
  The previous comment described 8 as the "sweet spot on modern
  workstations" — accurate for the original 4-8 core target, but
  on the 32+ core ingest hosts now used to rebuild large corpora
  the writer was bottlenecked on the 8-thread cap while machine
  cores sat idle.  32 puts the cap closer to the actual
  per-machine ceiling (disk + merge cost).  The full
  thread-count is now always clamped to the lower of system
  parallelism and this cap, so smaller hosts still get correct
  behaviour without modification.

  Operators can lower (or raise) the cap at runtime via the new
  `CASS_TANTIVY_MAX_WRITER_THREADS` env var.  The
  `dotenvy::var()` lookup, parse-as-usize, and `> 0` filter all
  match the existing pattern used by every other CASS_* knob in
  this crate (CASS_WRITER_HEAP_PER_THREAD_BYTES,
  CASS_MIN_WRITER_HEAP_BYTES, etc.).  When unset/invalid we fall
  back to the new 32-thread default.

CASS_BULK_LOAD_MIN_SEGMENTS_PER_MERGE: 32 → 256
  The bulk-load policy delays background merges until enough
  segments accumulate; the prior 32-segment threshold turned out
  to be too low on large rebuilds — merges still fired during
  the document-streaming phase and burned CPU that would
  otherwise have gone to indexing.  256 lets a typical multi-
  million-doc rebuild finish ingestion before the first merge
  cycle, then merge cleanly afterwards.

Use Writer::run with UserOperation::Add for batched adds
  Replaced the per-document `self.writer.add_document(d)` loop
  with `self.writer.run(prepared.into_iter().map(UserOperation::Add))`.
  Tantivy's `Writer::run` enqueues a contiguous batch of
  operations under a single internal lock acquisition, eliminating
  the per-doc overhead that was visible in flame graphs at high
  writer-thread counts.  Both the small-batch (sequential) and
  large-batch (rayon-parallel) prep paths now collect into a
  `Vec<TantivyDocument>` first and feed a single `run()` call,
  which removes the prior code duplication around the
  `add_document` loop.

  Required adding `UserOperation` to the tantivy imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
